commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an agent role evaluation CLI/library, two package scripts, a scheduled/manual read-only contract-catalog workflow, and focused script tests.

PR surface: Tests +346, Config +34, Other +1341. Total +1721 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. for the feature request as a whole; the blocking findings are source-reproducible by comparing the new script checks with current config docs and `src/config/io.ts`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blockers are narrow script repairs with clear files and tests; maintainer judgment is still needed afterward for the daily workflow surface.

target.title: Add agent role eval harness
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds a minimal deterministic agent role evaluation harness: CLI, reusable contract catalog, tests, package scripts, and a read-only contract-catalog workflow.
- Creates a clean prerequisite for follow-up Browser Steward runtime-enforcement work without carrying the older 49-commit / 300-file stacked branch.
- Intentionally excludes the secret-bearing/live workflow path; live eval automation is split out for separate maintainer review.
- Intentionally out of scope: Browser Steward runtime enforcement, unrelated dashboard/music/backup/SNES work, generated bundle hashes, and any credential/session mutation.
- Review focus: deterministic harness boundaries, workflow validity, test coverage, and the fact that the diff is limited to the minimal prerequisite surface.

## Linked context

Related: https://github.com/SnowBelt/openclaw/pull/1
Related: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93018

Requested by: local OpenClaw agent-build audit workflow to unblock Browser Steward upstream integration safely.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: The Browser Steward runtime-enforcement PR was blocked by an unsafe prerequisite branch containing 49 commits and 300 files. This PR provides the minimal reusable deterministic agent-role-eval harness needed as a clean upstream prerequisite.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `codex/agent-role-eval-harness-clean`, based on current `origin/main`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm agents:eval:contracts`; `node scripts/agent-role-eval.mjs --contracts-only --json`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 .github/workflows/agent-role-evals.yml scripts/agent-role-eval.mjs scripts/lib/agent-role-evals.mjs test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts package.json`; `git diff --check -- .github/workflows/agent-role-evals.yml package.json scripts test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts`; `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-precommit-venv/bin:$PATH pnpm check:workflows`; `env NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build`.
- Evidence after fix: Local terminal output showed the contract catalog passing with 51 contracts and 8 critical contracts, the focused test file passing, `zizmor` workflow audit passing, and `pnpm build` completing successfully.
- Observed result after fix: The harness validates deterministically, the workflow passes local workflow sanity, and the build succeeds while the branch remains one commit with five files changed.
- What was not tested: Secret-bearing live eval workflow behavior is not part of this PR and should be reviewed separately if reintroduced.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: `pnpm check:workflows` required a temporary `/tmp` Python virtualenv for `pre-commit`; no repo-tracked dependency files were changed for that local tool setup.
- Before evidence: The previous upstream Browser Steward PR targeted `main` directly and showed an oversized stacked diff, proving the old prerequisite path was too large for focused review.

## Tests and validation

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `pnpm agents:eval:contracts`
- `node scripts/agent-role-eval.mjs --contracts-only --json`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 .github/workflows/agent-role-evals.yml scripts/agent-role-eval.mjs scripts/lib/agent-role-evals.mjs test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts package.json`
- `git diff --check -- .github/workflows/agent-role-evals.yml package.json scripts test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts`
- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-precommit-venv/bin:$PATH pnpm check:workflows`
- `env NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192 pnpm build`

Regression coverage added:
- `test/scripts/agent-role-eval.test.ts` covers contract catalog validity, prompt scoring, config path resolution, configured-agent static checks, canonical omitted-agentDir state resolution, and deterministic workflow safety checks.

Known validation notes:
- First `pnpm check:workflows` failed because local `pre-commit` was missing; reran successfully using a temporary `/tmp` venv.
- First `pnpm build` failed because sandboxing blocked the repo-local heavy-check lock under Git metadata; reran successfully with escalated local filesystem access.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, adds package scripts and a scheduled/manual read-only workflow.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No secret-bearing or live workflow behavior remains in this PR. The workflow has read-only contents permission and runs deterministic catalog validation only.

Highest-risk area: recurring repository automation from the scheduled contract-catalog workflow.

Mitigation: The workflow is read-only, disables checkout credential persistence, does not reference `secrets.*`, and only runs `pnpm agents:eval:contracts`; local workflow sanity, `zizmor`, focused tests, GitHub CI, and build passed.

## Current review state

Current status: ClawSweeper is non-blocking and ready for maintainer review; current-head GitHub CI is green/skipped/neutral.

Next action: maintainer acceptance of the read-only scheduled workflow, then merge this prerequisite before opening the Browser Steward upstream PR.
target.author: snowbelt

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
